behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in segmentation logic itself (single parser boundary), not cross-module state handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; pure string->segments behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): call segmenter on "A=1\nB=2\ngh pr create" and assert 3 segments; catches newline-collapse bug, misses downstream trigger wiring. Current(Unit): include blank lines/comments and assert stable segment boundaries; catches tokenizer edge cases, misses persistence bugs. High(Integration): run full hook classifier pipeline; catches wiring too, but not required for this behavior. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration+ because behavior text is only about segmentation.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the segmentation function with multiline shell blocks (assignments + command), asserting each newline statement becomes a distinct segment and no monolithic segment is produced.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: incident failure is at module handoff (segmentation/classification), not just one regex. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required to detect trigger text; in-process wiring is enough. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) single decision pipeline only. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): test classifier on already-isolated "gh pr create" segment; catches classifier typo, misses newline handoff bug. Current(Integration): feed raw multiline block through segmenter+trigger detector and assert PR-create trigger; catches exact incident. High(System): execute real shell/gh; catches env quirks, but unnecessary for stated failure. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is recognition of command pattern, not real gh execution.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring segmenter + trigger classifier: input multiline block with variable assignments then `gh pr create`, assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: negative-case correctness depends on same segmentation->classification handoff as behavior 2. REAL-INFRA: no real gh call required; text classification only. Integration-brake: no required OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): classifier given single segment `gh issue create` -> not PR; catches keyword confusion, misses multiline handoff regressions. Current(Integration): raw multiline assignments + `gh issue create` through full local pipeline, assert non-PR trigger; catches false-positive from segment collapse/order bugs. High(System): run real shell/gh; extra infra not required for this failure mode. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is deterministic command-type discrimination.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with multiline command block (same structure as PR case but ending `gh issue create`) through segmenter+detector, asserting it is not classified as PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is reconstruction logic plus persistence adapter/state writer interaction. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem write/read in temp dir is sufficient; no real external API required if identifier source is provided in hook payload/command context. Integration-brake: (a) no mandatory real subprocess/network in behavior text, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): test identifier parser only; catches parse bug, misses persistence contract bugs. Current(Integration): run trigger handling path end-to-end with temp filesystem and assert PR id persisted in state artifact; catches missing/incorrect state writes. High(System): involve real `gh` subprocess/HTTP PR creation; broader but not required by stated behavior. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior says reconstruct/persist after detection, not create/fetch PR via real infra.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test trigger-handling path using temp filesystem: inject detected PR-create event with realistic metadata/output, assert persisted review-state contains the expected PR identifier fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an inter-module contract (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer). Unit tests on either side can miss schema/field mismatches. REAL-INFRA: temp-file contract test is enough; no real OS primitives beyond normal local filesystem. Integration-brake: no real network/subprocess requirement, no AI/ML dependence, no multi-agent sequence. Candidate tests: Low(Unit): validate writer schema object shape only; catches local schema drift, misses reader compatibility. Current(Integration): write state via post-hook path, then invoke pre-hook enforcement read path and assert enforcement decision uses that state; catches real contract break. High(System): run hooks via real CLI session/subprocess; useful smoke coverage but not minimum. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text targets read/write compatibility, not subprocess/HTTP behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration contract test between hooks: execute post-hook state write for detected PR trigger in temp workspace, then execute pre-hook enforcement read path and assert it successfully recognizes and enforces the gate from that state.
